title: AST Vehicle Collision: Fairbanks [AK25098740]
summary: On 09/27/2025 at 1745, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a vehicle collision at 2395 Old Chena Ridge Rd. The complainant stated the driver pinned a man into the dumpster with a black Chevrolet Silverado. After arrival on scene, Chena Goldstream EMS provided aid to victim who suffered injuri...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Rainy Days
summary: The rainy, windy weather is a good time for long meetings indoors (ideally with a hot cup of coffee). But I got out of the office—and out of town—to Sitka for Southeast Conference and the Village Public Safety Officer graduation at the Public Safety Academy. The post Rainy Days appeared first on Ala...

title: AST ATV Fatality: Brevig Mission [AK25098520]
summary: On September 27, 2025, at 1:27 AM, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a single-vehicle ATV collision in the Village of Brevig Mission. The two occupants of the ATV were both deceased. They were identified as 29-year-old Tommy Iyapana and 25-year-old Maude Adam, both of Brevig Mission. AST is...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: to Brevig Mission for further investigation

title: AST Murder 2: Wasilla [AK25080332]
summary: Update: On August 11, 2025, Alaska State Troopers responded to a residence in Wasilla for CPR in progress on a child. The child was transported to an area hospital and eventually succumbed to the injuries. An autopsy ruled the death a homicide. On September 25, 2025, a Palmer Grand Jury returned a t...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Assault: Fairbanks, AK [AK25098631]
summary: On 09/27/2025 at approximately 1330hrs, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of a man tailgating and brandishing a pistol in a newer black Toyota Tacoma. This occurred in the area of West Athena Drive. It was as an older white male (50-60s) with a gray mustache and a black ballcap. If you have any information, please contact the Alaska State Troopers at 907-451-5100 (caller can remain anonymous).
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Alaska’s Bristol Bay sockeye run and harvest increased this year, with fish sizes a bit bigger
summary: A spawning male sockeye salmon is seen in July 2010 in the Wood River, part of the Bristol Bay watershed. Bristol Bay is the site of the world's largest sockeye salmon runs. This year's Bristol Bay run was the 11th in a row with more than 50 million sockeye salmon, also known as red salmon. (Photo by Thomas Quinn/University of Washington, provided by the Environmental Protection Agency) The commercial salmon harvest in Alaska’s Bristol Bay, site of the world’s largest sockeye salmon runs, held a mixture of good news and bad n...

title: Haines Candidate Forum – Listen Here

Moderators Will Steinfeld and Melinda Munson questioned Assembly and Planning Commission candidates. Listen to the full broadcast here! The post Haines Candidate Forum – Listen Here appeared first on...

title: Alaska Supreme Court allows personal watercraft in Homer’s Kachemak Bay, reversing lower court
summary: Kachemak Bay waves roll into Homer on March 18, 2019. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) This article was updated at 7:50 p.m. Friday with comment from Cook Inletkeeper. The Alaska Supreme Court ruled Friday that the Alaska Department of Fish and Game may allow personal watercraft, commonly called Jet Skis, in Kachemak Bay south of Homer. The unanimous decision by the five-member court overturned a lower-court judge who had ruled in 2023 that the state could not eliminate a Jet Ski ban on...

title: KHNS Annual Meeting Tues 9/30 in Skagway & Wed 10/22 in Haines!

title: Medals awarded for Wounded Knee Massacre won’t be rescinded, Hegseth announces
summary: Plastic flowers and American flags are placed atop graves at the Wounded Knee Memorial and cemetery on June 30, 2024. (Makenzie Huber/South Dakota Searchlight) U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced Thursday that Medals of Honor awarded to U.S. soldiers for their role in the Wounded Knee Massacre will not be rescinded. The massacre occurred on Dec. 29, 1890. Lakota peo...
